inline_comments.message:
This is only a concern of the EtsiCatalogUrlProvider class so define it in there. 
Its the job of EtsiCatalogUrlProvider to take care of generating the url for etsi catalog manager, anything related to that shouldnt be leaking out into other classes

I wouldnt be a fan of using the constant from the implementation in the test.
If somebody changes the value of the constant, the necessity to update the test case highlights that the change is something that has external implications
Subject comment... the value is mocked in the test class so test is not covering any external implications

Changing PACKAGE_MANAGEMENT_BASE_URL would change the url of the endpoint we expose for package management and would be a backwards incompatible api change, the tests verify the exposed url by invoking it. 

Any change to the default endpoint used for etsi catalog manager has implications for any deployment where the default value is being used. 
The tests verify the url the vnfm adapter will invoke by default for the etsi catalog manager.

One of the reasons for CI is to verify your external api does not change inadvertently, changes to either of these values has implications externally

"Changing PACKAGE_MANAGEMENT_BASE_URL would change the url of the endpoint we expose for package management " Yes i understand that
"would be a backwards incompatible api change," How would that be backward incompatible ?
We mock the URL in this test and make sure default value is called and if the worry is that someone will change that then EtsiCatalogUrlProvider should have its own test rather then this test class test every single thing in one test class 

"One of the reasons for CI is to verify your external api does not change inadvertently, changes to either of these values has implications externally"
how extracting that to contants breaking that?

PACKAGE_MANAGEMENT_BASE_URL:
Changing the endpoint is backwards incompatible because any clients will have to be updated to use the new endpoint.
If you use the actual value for the endpoint in the test, rather than the constant used in the implementation, then any change in the constant will result in a failed test case, which would hopefully highlight to the implementer or reviewer the consequences of the change.

ETSI_CATALOG_MANAGER_DEFAULT_ENDPOINT:
Yes, the concern in somebody could change it. I dont see any reason why we would need a separate test class for that when it can be tested through the api (as it already is).

Using the constants from the implementation in the test means you are just verifying against what the adapter is using rather than against what the adapter should be using.
For example, the package management endpoint must be "/vnfpkgm/v1" to comply with the SOL003 spec. Using the constant here in the test class means you are verifying the endpoint is whatever the value of the constant is, rather than verifying it is the correct value of "/vnfpkgm/v1".

If someone is change the non constant value with understanding the implication of it, would change the test as well. for these sort of thing, we have code review processes.... as mentioned if the worry is someone somehow change the constant with realizing  what are the affect of it then as mentioned before that should be cover EtsiCatalogUrlProvider Test which will force user not to change the default value.

lets take this offline

This needs to be read in as an external configuration parameter because at can be changed by deployment. 
look at other config params for examples of now deployment specific addresses are build up e.g ./resources/config/mso/mso-docker.json (this is in OOM)
   ...
      "adaptersVnfRestEndpoint": "http://{{.Values.service.name}}:{{.Values.service.internalPort}}/vnfs/rest/v1/vnfs"
   ....

After discussion.
There shouldn't be a default of so if the parameter isn't set it is a failure.

Please test these scenarios through the api, i.e through the package management endpoint, as is done for the rest of the test cases.
This will allow you to verify the return code to the user (i.e. the VNFM) is in accordance with the SOl003 spec resulting in a more complete test than just verifying the constructor of the EtsiCatalogUrlProvider class in isolation

This constant is part of the code we are trying to test. The value assigned to it is a part of what we are trying to verify.
By verifying its value against itself you are not really verifying its value at all
Can't test that as api ... if url is null or empty it will fail JVM to startup
i didn't add this... plus that is what constant is for, in my opinion this is correct instead of duplicating it the value...
well then remove the code - why introduce code and test cases for a scenario that cannot happen
These scenario can happen if user put invalid url in override.yaml file
